nativeRange.habitat: Streams and slow-moving freshwater bodies in Sri Lanka; tolerates a wide range of conditions including hard water. Also introduced and naturalized in parts of Florida (USA).

avoidWith: Silver Dollars and other plant-eating fish

summary: A hardy Sri Lankan rosette plant and one of the most popular Cryptocoryne species in the hobby. Generally beginner-friendly, with multiple color forms (Green, Brown, Red, 'Mi Oya', 'Tropica', 'Florida Sunset').
careNotes: Cryptocoryne wendtii is widely considered the most popular Cryptocoryne species in the freshwater planted-tank hobby and is generally regarded as beginner-friendly. It is native to Sri Lanka (with a naturalized introduced population in Florida, USA) and forms compact rosettes of leaves that vary widely in color and texture depending on the named variety: Green is the standard form with smooth bright-green leaves; Brown and 'Mi Oya' (named after a Sri Lankan river) tend toward bronze-brown coloration; Red and 'Florida Sunset' are reddish/pink-flushed cultivars; 'Tropica' is a Tropica-bred cultivar with darkly hammered/textured leaves. Mature size is typically 10-30 cm tall with rosettes 8-20 cm wide; the green form tends toward the larger end of the range while compact cultivars like 'Tropica' stay smaller. Lighting requirements are flexible — low to medium light is sufficient and produces vibrant coloration, while higher light can accelerate growth and intensify colors but may also encourage algae. CO2 is not strictly necessary but is reported to improve growth rate, leaf size, and color saturation. Cryptocoryne wendtii is a HEAVY ROOT FEEDER — nutrient uptake happens primarily through the root zone, so a nutrient-rich substrate (aqua soil) and/or regular use of root tabs near the root mass is strongly recommended. Liquid water-column fertilization is helpful but secondary. Plant by burying the roots in substrate up to the crown (the point where leaves emerge from the root mass) — do NOT bury the crown itself or it may rot. CRYPT MELT WARNING: when newly planted or after a major water-parameter change, Cryptocoryne wendtii frequently sheds most or all of its existing leaves in a process called 'crypt melt'. This is normal and the plant typically regrows new leaves adapted to the new conditions within 2-4 weeks; do not remove the rhizome and roots during a melt event. To minimize melt, avoid moving an established plant once it has settled in. Temperature consensus across sources is 22-28 °C for ideal aquarium use; the plant tolerates a wider 15-30 °C range per Aquasabi. pH 6.0-7.5 is the practical aquarium range (tolerates pH 5-8 per Aquasabi); gH and kH ranges are very forgiving, with the species thriving in hard water. Source disagreement note: Buce Plant lists native range as Borneo, which is incorrect — Tropica, Wikipedia, Aquarium Co-Op, AquariumPlants.com, and Aquasabi all confirm Sri Lanka. Buce Plant also lists leaf size as 2-3 inches, which refers to leaf length rather than total plant height; the 10-30 cm height range is confirmed by Tropica and Aquasabi. This entry covers the species-level baseline; cultivar-specific entries (e.g., 'Tropica' as a separately tracked cultivar) may be added later if granularity is required.
breedingNotes: Propagated primarily by runners (stolons) that produce daughter plants alongside the parent; once the daughter plant has developed several leaves and a healthy root system, it can be carefully separated from the runner and replanted. Rhizome division is also possible: cut a healthy rhizome into sections, each retaining leaves and roots, and replant. Cryptocoryne wendtii can also flower (producing the spathe-and-spadix inflorescence typical of Araceae) when grown emersed, but seed propagation is not practical in the home aquarium.

primary.name: Tropica
primary.notes: Primary source page for Cryptocoryne wendtii 'Green'. Confirmed Sri Lankan origin, height 10-30+ cm, low light demand, low CO2 demand, medium growth, easy difficulty, and rosette form.

additional.name: Buce Plant
additional.notes: Cross-checked light (low-to-medium), CO2 (not necessary but recommended), pH range (6.0-7.5), slow-to-medium growth, easy difficulty, foreground-to-midground placement, melt warning, and quality-aquarium-soil recommendation. NOTE: Buce Plant lists native range as Borneo, which conflicts with 4 other sources (Sri Lanka); their stated leaf-size of 2-3 inches refers to leaf length, not total plant height.

additional.name: Aquasabi
additional.notes: Authoritative source for temperature (15-30 °C), pH (5-8), gH (0-30 °dGH), kH (1-18 °dKH), and 10-30 cm height confirmation. Aquasabi is a European specialist retailer with reliable Tropica-aligned parameter data.

additional.name: Aquarium Co-Op
additional.notes: Cross-checked easy difficulty, low-light tolerance, midground placement, and provided the standard explanation of crypt melt as a normal acclimation response (2-4 week recovery).

additional.name: Wikipedia
additional.notes: Used for taxonomy (Araceae, Alismatales), confirmation of Sri Lankan origin and Florida introduction, list of named varieties ('Tropica', 'Mi Oya', var. jahnelii, var. krauteri, var. nana, var. rubella), and propagation by runners.

additional.name: AquariumPlants.com
additional.notes: Cross-checked Sri Lankan native range, tolerance of low-to-high light (thrives under medium), no supplemental CO2 required, beginner-friendly, and vigorous growth once established.
